perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all such Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of Confidential Information. The level of care is left to Recipient's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause sets a clear standard of care ('at least the same degree... but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree'). The modified text removes this defined standard, delegating the level of care to the Recipient's discretion. This introduces ambiguity and contradicts Section 8, which allows for equitable remedies like injunctive relief if the Disclosing Party suffers irreparable harm.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient hereby further agrees that, in the event of its breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party would suffer irreparable harm and the Disclosing Party’s remedies shall include, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity, equitable remedies such as specific performance and injunctive relief and shall not be limited to monetary damages. Specifically, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief prohibiting such violation.
perturbation.justification: The original text establishes a specific standard of care for protecting confidential information, while the changed text delegates the level of care to the Recipient's discretion. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which allows the Disclosing Party to seek equitable remedies, including injunctive relief, in the event of a breach or threatened breach. By removing the defined standard of care, the changed text introduces ambiguity, making it more difficult to determine whether a breach has occurred and undermining the Disclosing Party's ability to obtain injunctive relief, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as the change removes key items that are later required or referenced.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall not, and s hall cause its Representatives not to, (a) disclose all or any portion of Confidential Information to any third parties (other than to its Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information to assist Recipient in its evaluation of the Purpose ), or (b) use or permit any Confidential Information to be accessed or used for any purpose other than for the evalua tion of the Purpose . Recipient will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement caused by its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to third parties, but the extent of responsibility for Representatives' breaches will be determined on a case-by-case basis.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states Recipient *will be* responsible for breaches by Representatives. The modified text introduces uncertainty by stating responsibility will be determined case-by-case. This contradicts the clarity of the original clause and affects the enforceability of Section 8 (equitable remedies).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient hereby further agrees that, in the event of its breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party would suffer irreparable harm and the Disclosing Party’s remedies shall include, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity, equitable remedies such as specific performance and injunctive relief and shall not be limited to monetary damages. Specifically, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief prohibiting such violation.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states the Recipient will be responsible for breaches caused by its Representatives. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating the responsibility for Representatives' breaches will be determined on a case-by-case basis. This change directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that in the event of a breach, the Disclosing Party would suffer irreparable harm, and the Disclosing Party’s remedies shall include equitable remedies such as specific performance and injunctive relief, which shall not be limited to monetary damages. The change introduces ambiguity about the recipient's responsibility, which weakens the enforceability of equitable remedies, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, w ithout the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, Recipient shall not disclose to any person or entity (other than its Representatives who are - 2 - involved in its evalua tion of the Purpose ) any information with respect to the occurrence or content of any discussions or negotiations between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall endeavor not to disclose information about discussions or negotiations, but this is not a strict prohibition.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses the phrase 'shall not disclose', which represents a clear prohibition. The modified text uses 'shall endeavor not to disclose', weakening the obligation and making it less enforceable. This directly contradicts the expectation of confidentiality established throughout the agreement and weakens section 8 regarding remedies in case of a breach or threatened breach
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient hereby further agrees that, in the event of its breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party would suffer irreparable harm and the Disclosing Party’s remedies shall include, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity, equitable remedies such as specific performance and injunctive relief and shall not be limited to monetary damages. Specifically, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief prohibiting such violation.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits disclosure ('shall not disclose'). The changed text weakens this to 'shall endeavor not to disclose', explicitly stating it 'is not a strict prohibition'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a strong, enforceable agreement with remedies for 'breach or threatened breach' and 'immediate injunctive relief'. The change fundamentally alters the obligation and thus creates a strong in-text contradiction by undermining the enforceability of the agreement and the availability of remedies.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient understands and agrees that neither the Disclosing Party nor any person or entity delivering the Confidential Information on behalf of Disclosing Party is making any representation or warrant y, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any information furnished or to be furnished by it to Recipient (any representation or warranty with respect thereto must be set forth or provided for in a definitive agreement between the parties, if any is entered into in connecti on with the Purpose or certification from Disclosing Party ).
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosing Party may provide representations or warranties, depending on the context and at their sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly disclaims any representations or warranties unless specified in a separate agreement. The modified text introduces the possibility of representations/warranties at the Disclosing Party's discretion, contradicting the initial disclaimer and creating potential liability where none existed. This uncertainty contradicts Section 6, which sought to limit liability of the Disclosing party
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient understands and agrees that neither the Disclosing Party nor any person or entity delivering the Confidential Information on behalf of Disclosing Party is making any representation or warrant y, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any information furnished or to be furnished by it to Recipient (any representation or warranty with respect thereto must be set forth or provided for in a definitive agreement between the parties, if any is entered into in connecti on with the Purpose or certification from Disclosing Party ).
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text state that the Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties unless in a definitive agreement. However, the changed text allows the Disclosing Party to provide representations or warranties at their discretion, directly contradicting the previous statement that such representations are only valid if in a separate agreement. This creates a contradiction because it makes the original disclaimer potentially unenforceable, as the Disclosing Party can now provide warranties outside of a formal agreement, which is against the initial disclaimer in both original and contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Upon request of the Disclosing Party (which request may be made at any time upon reasonable notice), Recipient will destroy (or, if stored electronically, purge) or deliver to the Disclosing Party all copies of written Confidential Information (except for that portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analysis, compilation, or other documents prepared by Recipient) in Recipient’s possession. That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analysis, compilations and other documents prepared by Recipient will continue to be subj ect to the terms of this Agreement or shall be destroyed (or, if stored electronically, purged).
perturbation.changed_text: Upon request of the Disclosing Party, Recipient will make reasonable efforts to return or destroy Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly dictates that Recipient *will* destroy or return Confidential Information upon request. The modified text weakens this obligation to 'make reasonable efforts,' introducing ambiguity and reducing the Disclosing Party's control over its Confidential Information. This directly diminishes the level of protection afforded by the agreement, implicitly contradicts Section 2 where recipient ensures to protect the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all such Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Recipient 'will destroy or deliver' Confidential Information upon request. The changed text weakens this to 'make reasonable efforts to return or destroy,' introducing ambiguity. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which requires the Recipient to 'protect and safeguard the confidentiality' with a specific degree of care. The 'reasonable efforts' standard is less stringent than the 'same degree of care,' creating a direct conflict regarding the level of protection afforded to the Confidential Information. This aligns with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition because essential language was removed or altered, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.
